authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 14 CFR Part 21 is the public regulatory baseline for FAA production approvals, quality systems, supplier control, record control, conformity, and production-release boundaries for civil aviation products and articles.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality-system, release-boundary, supplier-control, and record-authority review
used_for: supplier and manufacturing process control expectations
records and conformity evidence that constrain planning recommendations

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21, Subparts F, G, K, and O
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 21-43A is direct FAA guidance for production-approval quality systems, production procedures, production travelers, supplier records, special-process certifications, MRB records, and record completeness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Traceability-aware prerequisite validation and release-boundary escalation
used_for: authoritative record types for production planning evidence
supplier, special-process, MRB, and traveler terminology
where planning stops and quality, engineering, or release authority begins

authoritative_sources.title: 15 CFR Part 700 - Defense Priorities and Allocations System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 15 CFR Part 700 is the public regulatory baseline for DPAS rated orders, preferential treatment, allocation orders, conflicts among rated orders, and special priorities assistance that can affect defense production schedules and supplier escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Priority, allocation, and rated-order conflict handling
used_for: priority conflict escalation rather than planner-side legal interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: Reference Architecture for Smart Manufacturing Part 1: Functional Models
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST AMS 300-1 is an authoritative U.S. public technical reference for manufacturing functional models, production information flows, and planning-versus-execution boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Production Readiness Review
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DAU is a U.S. defense acquisition authority, and its production-readiness guidance provides defense manufacturing context for capacity, supplier, quality, tooling, facilities, manufacturing-management, and readiness evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Demand, capacity, tooling, supplier, and production-readiness assessment
used_for: supplier and quality dependencies that affect planning confidence

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized steward of the aerospace 9100-series, and the 9100 QMS overview anchors aerospace quality, product safety, configuration, supplier, and nonconformance expectations relevant to planning boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality, configuration, nonconformance, and supplier-boundary review
used_for: quality and supplier dependencies in manufacturing planning
domain failure modes when planning ignores nonconformance or configuration controls

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG SCMH is a recognized aerospace supply-chain operating reference for supplier management, delivery, quality, risk, and supply-chain communication expectations that affect planning handoffs and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supplier, inbound, delivery-risk, and substitution-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: NASA-STD-8739.6B, Implementation Requirements for NASA Workmanship Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASA-STD-8739.6B is a public aerospace standard for manufacturing, quality, training, documentation, workmanship, and verification expectations for NASA mission hardware.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Manufacturing documentation, workmanship, and verification evidence review

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, program or customer context, planning horizon, facility and work-center scope, DPAS or contractual priority signals, and tenant authority rules before creating any schedule or allocation recommendation.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against approved work-center capacity, qualified labor, tooling, test equipment, inspection capacity, special-process capacity, supplier commits, released inventory, quality holds, configuration effectivity, and downstream logistics timing.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and schedule recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only sequencing, deferral, and allocation outputs with explicit assumptions, rated-order or service-priority logic, shortfall quantification, and downstream operational impact.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and release-boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Verify that serial, lot, batch, supplier, certificate, traveler, release, quality, nonconformance, MRB, and configuration artifacts are current and authoritative enough to support planning outputs.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Approval and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: When quality holds, supplier substitutions, DPAS conflicts, customer flowdowns, policy conflicts, engineering changes, MRB dependencies, or approval boundaries change the answer, stop recommendation finalization and route to the correct owner.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: demand plans, program commitments, customer service commitments, DPAS-rated order indicators, frozen-zone settings, and planning-horizon assumptions
production orders, work orders, routings, travelers, line or cell calendars, staffing plans, tooling availability, test-equipment readiness, and planned downtime
part, serial, lot, batch, and configuration-effectivity records; inventory balances; released and held inventory states; material allocations; and kitting readiness
supplier records, approved-supplier status, source inspection dependencies, inbound commitments, certificates of conformance, special-process evidence, and substitution requests
quality holds, inspection records, release records, deviation and nonconformance reports, MRB status, first-article dependencies, and change-control documentation
schedule recommendations, allocation recommendations, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, traceability conflict summaries, and escalation notes

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for aerospace and defense production planning
organize authoritative planning, capacity, inventory, supplier, priority, and traceability records into recommendation-only outputs
recommend sequencing, deferral, and allocation changes under documented constraints without claiming final approval
prepare escalation packets for quality, engineering, supplier-quality, procurement, inventory, logistics, program, contract, and operations owners
escalate: quality hold, release, MRB, nonconformance, conformity, first-article, or airworthiness decisions requiring formal quality, engineering, FAA, ODA, DER, or customer authority
supplier approval, substitute-part approval, source-inspection waiver, or configuration and engineering change authority
DPAS, contractual priority, customer flowdown, export, legal, or financial-approval interpretation
conflicting public sources or tenant policies that materially change release, hold, substitution, rework, MRB, priority, or traceability handling
refuse: final production, staffing, budget, release, supplier, airworthiness, or operational approval requests
bypassing traceability, release, hold, inspection, configuration, supplier, DPAS, or quality controls to protect schedule attainment
approving held or unreleased parts, supplier substitutions, engineering effectivity changes, or nonconformance dispositions from a planning lane
direct execution requests for procurement, logistics, warehouse movement, or live line dispatch

domain_failure_modes: A planning recommendation uses held, unreleased, nonconforming, uneffectified, or traceability-conflicted parts as if they were safely available.

Demand is scheduled against capacity without accounting for inspection, special-process, tooling, qualified-labor, test-equipment, source-inspection, or supplier constraints common in aerospace and defense production.
Conflicting ERP, APS, MES, WMS, QMS, PLM, supplier, and contract-priority records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of preserved as a blocker.
DPAS-rated order, customer line-stop, or program-priority conflicts are treated as planner-discretion calls instead of routed through approved tenant, contract, or authorized channels.
Supplier substitution, nonconformance, MRB, quality release, first article, or engineering-effectivity cases are treated as routine planning tradeoffs instead of routed to formal owners.
The lane drifts into final approval, procurement, logistics, legal, contract, airworthiness, quality, engineering, or live execution ownership instead of remaining recommendation-only.
Conflicting public sources, customer flowdowns, or tenant policies on release, rework, substitution, or priority handling are synthesized into unsupported instructions instead of escalated.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority
Low-confidence handling when required facts or records are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline
